content.text: How can I create a detached clone of this entity instance without duplicating its primary key dependencies?

To implement an efficient shallow copy, we first convert the current object into a dictionary using `to_dict()`. We then remove the unique identifier before reconstructing a new instance.

```python
def copy(self):
        '''
        Creates a shallow copy of the given entity (any entities that can be
        retrieved from a OneToMany relationship will not be copied).
        '''
        x = self.to_dict()
        x.pop(self._pkey)
        return self.__class__(**x)
```

This method effectively creates a deep-enough replica for simple attributes but maintains references (shallow) to complex relationships. Note that fields from one-to-many relationships are intentionally excluded here.
If this entity contains mutable defaults, like lists or dictionaries, will their contents be copied?

Since the copy is shallow, any nested mutable objects referenced by name will still point to the original instance. To clone those deeply embedded items, you would need a deep copy mechanism instead of direct dict unpacking.
That clarifies how object isolation works here.
